docs.title: Omni-GitHub
docs.text: GitHub providers and skills for the PlexusOne ecosystem.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Omni-GitHub provides two main components:

Package Description omnistorage/ GitHub as a storage backend for omnistorage-core omniskill/github/ GitHub skill for AI agents (issues, PRs, code search)

docs.title: Quick Start
Storage Provider
docs.text:
• 📄 Read and write files to any branch
  • ⚡ Batch multiple file operations into a single atomic commit
  • 📂 List files with prefix filtering
  • 🏢 GitHub Enterprise support

docs.title: GitHub Skill
docs.text:
• 🎫 Issue management (list, create, update, comment)
  • 🔀 Pull request operations (list, get, comment)
  • 🔍 Code and issue search
  • 🏢 GitHub Enterprise support

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go get github.com/plexusone/omni-github

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Go 1.26 or later
  • GitHub personal access token

docs.title: Related Projects
docs.text:
• omnistorage-core - Storage abstraction library
  • omniskill - Skill interface library
  • omniagent - AI agent runtime

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Go 1.26 or later
  • GitHub personal access token with appropriate scopes

docs.title: Installing the Package
docs.text: go get github.com/plexusone/omni-github

docs.title: For Storage Provider
docs.text:
The storage provider needs access to repository contents:

Scope Required For repo Full repository access (private repos) public_repo Public repository access only

docs.title: For GitHub Skill
docs.text:
The GitHub skill needs access to issues and pull requests:

Scope Required For repo Full repository access read:org List organization repositories

docs.title: Environment Variable (Recommended)
docs.text: export GITHUB_TOKEN="ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxx"

docs.title: GitHub CLI
docs.text:
If you have the GitHub CLI installed:

gh auth token

docs.title: GitHub Enterprise
docs.text:
For GitHub Enterprise, you'll also need to set the base URL:

export GITHUB_API_URL="https://github.mycompany.com/api/v3"

docs.title: Verifying Installation
docs.text:
package main

import (
    "context"
    "fmt"
    "os"

    "github.com/plexusone/omni-github/omniskill/github"
)

func main() {
    skill := github.New(github.Config{
        Token: os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"),
    })

    if err := skill.Init(context.Background()); err != nil {
        fmt.Printf("Error: %v\n", err)
        os.Exit(1)
    }

    fmt.Printf("GitHub skill initialized: %s\n", skill.Name())
}

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Quick Start - Get started with examples
  • Storage Provider - Use GitHub as storage
  • GitHub Skill - Use GitHub tools with AI agents

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: This guide shows how to use both the storage provider and GitHub skill.

docs.title: Reading Files
docs.text:
package main

import (
    "context"
    "io"
    "log"
    "os"

    "github.com/plexusone/omni-github/omnistorage"
)

func main() {
    backend, err := omnistorage.New(omnistorage.Config{
        Owner:  "plexusone",
        Repo:   "omnistorage-core",
        Branch: "main",
        Token:  os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"),
    })
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
    defer backend.Close()

    ctx := context.Background()

    // Read a file
    r, err := backend.NewReader(ctx, "README.md")
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
    defer r.Close()

    data, _ := io.ReadAll(r)
    log.Println(string(data))
}

docs.title: Writing Files
docs.text:
// Write a new file (creates a commit)
w, err := backend.NewWriter(ctx, "docs/example.txt")
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
w.Write([]byte("Hello, GitHub!"))
w.Close() // Commits the file

docs.title: Batch Operations
docs.text:
// Create a batch for atomic multi-file commits
batch, err := backend.NewBatch(ctx, "Update multiple files")
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

batch.Write("file1.txt", []byte("content 1"))
batch.Write("file2.txt", []byte("content 2"))
batch.Delete("old-file.txt")

if err := batch.Commit(); err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
package main

import (
    "context"
    "log"
    "os"

    "github.com/plexusone/omni-github/omniskill/github"
)

func main() {
    ctx := context.Background()

    skill := github.New(github.Config{
        Token:        os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"),
        DefaultOwner: "plexusone",
        DefaultRepo:  "omni-github",
    })

    if err := skill.Init(ctx); err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
    defer skill.Close()

    // Get available tools
    for _, tool := range skill.Tools() {
        log.Printf("Tool: %s - %s", tool.Name(), tool.Description())
    }
}

docs.title: With OmniAgent
docs.text:
import (
    "github.com/plexusone/omniagent/agent"
    "github.com/plexusone/omni-github/omniskill/github"
)

// Create GitHub skill
githubSkill := github.New(github.Config{
    Token: os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"),
})

// Register with agent
agent, err := agent.New(config,
    agent.WithSkills(githubSkill),
)

docs.title: Calling Tools Directly
docs.text:
// List open issues
listTool := skill.Tools()[0] // list_issues
result, err := listTool.Execute(ctx, map[string]any{
    "owner": "plexusone",
    "repo":  "omni-github",
    "state": "open",
})

// Create an issue
createTool := skill.Tools()[2] // create_issue
result, err := createTool.Execute(ctx, map[string]any{
    "owner":  "plexusone",
    "repo":   "omni-github",
    "title":  "Bug report",
    "body":   "Description of the bug",
    "labels": "bug,priority:high",
})

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Storage Configuration - Detailed configuration options
  • GitHub Skill Tools - Complete tool reference

docs.title: GitHub Skill
docs.text: The GitHub skill provides tools for managing issues, pull requests, and searching code on GitHub.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: import "github.com/plexusone/omni-github/omniskill/github"

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
type Config struct {
    // Token is the GitHub personal access token (required)
    Token string

    // BaseURL is the GitHub API base URL
    // Default: https://api.github.com
    // Set for GitHub Enterprise
    BaseURL string

    // DefaultOwner is the default repository owner
    DefaultOwner string

    // DefaultRepo is the default repository name
    DefaultRepo string
}

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
skill := github.New(github.Config{
    Token: os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"),
})

if err := skill.Init(ctx); err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
defer skill.Close()

// Register with agent
agent.RegisterSkill(skill)

docs.title: list_issues
docs.text:
List issues in a repository.

Parameter Type Required Description owner string No* Repository owner repo string No* Repository name state string No open, closed, or all (default: open) labels string No Comma-separated label names per_page integer No Results per page, max 100 (default: 30)

*Uses default if configured

docs.title: get_issue
docs.text:
Get details of a specific issue.

Parameter Type Required Description owner string No* Repository owner repo string No* Repository name number integer Yes Issue number

docs.title: create_issue
docs.text:
Create a new issue.

Parameter Type Required Description owner string No* Repository owner repo string No* Repository name title string Yes Issue title body string No Issue body (markdown) labels string No Comma-separated label names assignees string No Comma-separated usernames

docs.title: update_issue
docs.text:
Update an existing issue.

Parameter Type Required Description owner string No* Repository owner repo string No* Repository name number integer Yes Issue number title string No New title body string No New body state string No open or closed labels string No Comma-separated labels (replaces existing)

docs.title: add_issue_comment
docs.text:
Add a comment to an issue.

Parameter Type Required Description owner string No* Repository owner repo string No* Repository name number integer Yes Issue number body string Yes Comment body (markdown)

docs.title: list_pull_requests
docs.text:
List pull requests in a repository.

Parameter Type Required Description owner string No* Repository owner repo string No* Repository name state string No open, closed, or all (default: open) base string No Filter by base branch head string No Filter by head branch (user:branch) per_page integer No Results per page, max 100 (default: 30)

docs.title: get_pull_request
docs.text:
Get details of a specific pull request.

Parameter Type Required Description owner string No* Repository owner repo string No* Repository name number integer Yes PR number

docs.title: add_pull_request_comment
docs.text:
Add a comment to a pull request.

Parameter Type Required Description owner string No* Repository owner repo string No* Repository name number integer Yes PR number body string Yes Comment body (markdown)

docs.title: search_code
docs.text:
Search code across repositories.

Parameter Type Required Description query string Yes Search query (GitHub syntax) per_page integer No Results per page, max 100 (default: 30)

Example queries:

  • "TODO" repo:plexusone/omni-github - Search for TODO in a repo
  • "func main" language:go - Search Go main functions
  • "API_KEY" filename:.env - Search for API keys in .env files

docs.title: search_issues
docs.text:
Search issues and pull requests.

Parameter Type Required Description query string Yes Search query (GitHub syntax) per_page integer No Results per page, max 100 (default: 30)

Example queries:

  • is:issue is:open label:bug - Open bug issues
  • is:pr is:merged author:username - Merged PRs by user
  • "memory leak" is:issue - Issues mentioning memory leak

docs.title: GitHub Enterprise
docs.text:
skill := github.New(github.Config{
    Token:   os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"),
    BaseURL: "https://github.mycompany.com/api/v3",
})

docs.title: Default Repository
docs.text:
Set defaults to avoid repeating owner/repo:

skill := github.New(github.Config{
    Token:        os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"),
    DefaultOwner: "plexusone",
    DefaultRepo:  "omni-github",
})

// Now you can omit owner/repo
result, _ := listTool.Execute(ctx, map[string]any{
    "state": "open",
})

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
All tools return errors for:

  • Missing required parameters
  • API errors (rate limits, permissions, not found)
  • Network errors
result, err := tool.Execute(ctx, params)
if err != nil {
    // Handle error
    log.Printf("Tool failed: %v", err)
}

docs.title: Skills Overview
docs.text: The omniskill/ directory contains GitHub skills for AI agents. Skills implement the omniskill Skill interface, providing tools that agents can use.

docs.title: Available Skills
docs.text: Skill Package Description GitHub omniskill/github Issues, PRs, and code search

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
omni-github/
├── omnistorage/         # Storage provider
└── omniskill/
    └── github/          # GitHub skill (current)
    └── actions/         # GitHub Actions skill (planned)
    └── releases/        # Releases skill (planned)

docs.title: Using Skills with OmniAgent
docs.text:
import (
    "github.com/plexusone/omniagent/agent"
    "github.com/plexusone/omni-github/omniskill/github"
)

// Create skill
githubSkill := github.New(github.Config{
    Token: os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"),
})

// Initialize
if err := githubSkill.Init(ctx); err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

// Register with agent
agent, err := agent.New(config,
    agent.WithSkills(githubSkill),
)

docs.title: Using with OmniAgent Starter
docs.text:
For a batteries-included setup:

import starter "github.com/plexusone/omniagent-starter"

bundle := starter.Default(starter.Config{
    GitHubToken: os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"),
})

agent, err := agent.New(config,
    agent.WithSkills(bundle.Skills()...),
)

docs.title: Skill Interface
docs.text:
All skills implement the skill.Skill interface:

type Skill interface {
    Name() string
    Description() string
    Init(ctx context.Context) error
    Close() error
    Tools() []Tool
}

docs.title: GitHub Actions (omniskill/actions/)
docs.text:
• List workflow runs
  • Trigger workflows
  • Get workflow logs
  • Cancel runs

docs.title: Releases (omniskill/releases/)
docs.text:
• List releases
  • Create releases
  • Upload assets
  • Manage release notes

docs.title: Batch Operations
docs.text: For multiple file operations in a single commit, use the batch API. This is more efficient than individual writes and creates a cleaner commit history.

docs.title: Why Use Batches?
docs.text: Approach Commits API Calls Best For Individual writes 1 per file 1 per file Single file updates Batch operations 1 total 3-4 total Multiple file updates

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
// Create a batch with a commit message
batch, err := backend.NewBatch(ctx, "Update multiple files")
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

// Queue operations
batch.Write("file1.txt", []byte("content for file 1"))
batch.Write("file2.txt", []byte("content for file 2"))
batch.Delete("old-file.txt")

// Commit all changes atomically
if err := batch.Commit(); err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
The batch API uses Git's low-level APIs for efficiency:

  1. Get current tree - Fetches the current branch's tree SHA
  2. Create blobs - Creates blobs for new/updated file contents
  3. Create new tree - Creates a new tree with all changes
  4. Create commit - Creates a commit pointing to the new tree
  5. Update ref - Updates the branch to point to the new commit

This results in a single atomic commit with all changes.

docs.title: Mixed Operations
docs.text:
Batches support both writes and deletes:

batch, _ := backend.NewBatch(ctx, "Refactor: rename config files")

// Delete old files
batch.Delete("config/old-name.yaml")
batch.Delete("config/deprecated.yaml")

// Write new files
batch.Write("config/new-name.yaml", newConfig)
batch.Write("config/settings.yaml", settings)

batch.Commit()

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
If any operation in the batch fails, the entire batch is rolled back:

batch, err := backend.NewBatch(ctx, "Update files")
if err != nil {
    // Failed to initialize batch
    log.Fatal(err)
}

batch.Write("file1.txt", data1)
batch.Write("file2.txt", data2)

if err := batch.Commit(); err != nil {
    // Entire batch failed - no partial commits
    log.Printf("Batch failed: %v", err)
}

docs.title: Group Related Changes
docs.text:
// Good: Related changes in one batch
batch, _ := backend.NewBatch(ctx, "Add user feature")
batch.Write("models/user.go", userModel)
batch.Write("handlers/user.go", userHandler)
batch.Write("tests/user_test.go", userTest)
batch.Commit()

docs.title: Use Descriptive Commit Messages
docs.text:
// Good: Clear commit message
batch, _ := backend.NewBatch(ctx, "fix: resolve config parsing issue #123")

// Bad: Generic message
batch, _ := backend.NewBatch(ctx, "update files")

docs.title: Batch Size Limits
docs.text:
While there's no hard limit on batch size, consider:

  • GitHub API rate limits
  • Large batches may timeout
  • Recommended: < 100 files per batch
// For large updates, split into multiple batches
for i := 0; i < len(files); i += 50 {
    batch, _ := backend.NewBatch(ctx, fmt.Sprintf("Update batch %d", i/50+1))
    for _, f := range files[i:min(i+50, len(files))] {
        batch.Write(f.Path, f.Content)
    }
    batch.Commit()
}

docs.title: Config Struct
docs.text:
type Config struct {
    // Owner is the repository owner (required)
    Owner string

    // Repo is the repository name (required)
    Repo string

    // Branch is the branch name (default: "main")
    Branch string

    // Token is the GitHub personal access token (required)
    Token string

    // BaseURL is the API base URL (default: "https://api.github.com/")
    BaseURL string

    // UploadURL is the upload URL (default: "https://uploads.github.com/")
    UploadURL string

    // CommitMessage is the commit message template.
    // Use {path} as a placeholder for the file path.
    // Default: "Update {path} via omnistorage"
    CommitMessage string

    // CommitAuthor is the author for commits.
    // If nil, uses the authenticated user.
    CommitAuthor *CommitAuthor
}

type CommitAuthor struct {
    Name  string
    Email string
}

docs.title: Basic Configuration
docs.text:
backend, err := omnistorage.New(omnistorage.Config{
    Owner:  "myorg",
    Repo:   "myrepo",
    Branch: "main",
    Token:  os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"),
})

docs.title: Custom Commit Messages
docs.text:
backend, err := omnistorage.New(omnistorage.Config{
    Owner:         "myorg",
    Repo:          "myrepo",
    Token:         os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"),
    CommitMessage: "[bot] Update {path}",
    CommitAuthor: &omnistorage.CommitAuthor{
        Name:  "My Bot",
        Email: "bot@example.com",
    },
})

docs.title: GitHub Enterprise
docs.text:
backend, err := omnistorage.New(omnistorage.Config{
    Owner:     "myorg",
    Repo:      "myrepo",
    Branch:    "main",
    Token:     os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"),
    BaseURL:   "https://github.example.com/api/v3/",
    UploadURL: "https://github.example.com/uploads/",
})

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Configuration can be loaded from environment variables:

cfg := omnistorage.ConfigFromEnv()
backend, err := omnistorage.New(cfg)
Variable Fallback Description OMNISTORAGE_GITHUB_OWNER GITHUB_OWNER Repository owner OMNISTORAGE_GITHUB_REPO GITHUB_REPO Repository name OMNISTORAGE_GITHUB_BRANCH - Branch name (default: "main") OMNISTORAGE_GITHUB_TOKEN GITHUB_TOKEN Personal access token OMNISTORAGE_GITHUB_BASE_URL GITHUB_API_URL API base URL OMNISTORAGE_GITHUB_UPLOAD_URL - Upload URL OMNISTORAGE_GITHUB_COMMIT_MESSAGE - Commit message template OMNISTORAGE_GITHUB_COMMIT_AUTHOR_NAME - Commit author name OMNISTORAGE_GITHUB_COMMIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL - Commit author email

docs.title: Using the Registry
docs.text:
The backend automatically registers with the omnistorage registry:

import (
    omnistorage "github.com/plexusone/omnistorage-core/object"
    _ "github.com/plexusone/omni-github/omnistorage" // Register backend
)

backend, err := omnistorage.Open("github", map[string]string{
    "owner":  "myorg",
    "repo":   "myrepo",
    "branch": "main",
    "token":  os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"),
})

docs.title: Storage Provider Overview
docs.text: The omnistorage/ package implements the omnistorage-core Backend interface, allowing you to use GitHub repositories as a storage backend.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• 📄 Read and write files to any branch
  • ⚡ Batch multiple file operations into a single atomic commit
  • 📂 List files in directories with prefix filtering
  • ℹ️ Get file metadata (size, SHA1 hash)
  • 🗑️ Delete files from the repository
  • ⚙️ Configurable commit messages and author
  • 🏢 GitHub Enterprise support
  • 🔗 Automatic registration with OmniStorage registry

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
import "github.com/plexusone/omni-github/omnistorage"

backend, err := omnistorage.New(omnistorage.Config{
    Owner:  "myorg",
    Repo:   "myrepo",
    Branch: "main",
    Token:  os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"),
})
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
defer backend.Close()

docs.title: Supported Operations
docs.text: Operation Supported Notes NewReader ✅ Reads file content via Contents API NewWriter ✅ Creates/updates files (each write = 1 commit) NewBatch ✅ Atomic multi-file commits via Git Trees API Exists ✅ Checks if file/directory exists Delete ✅ Deletes files (each delete = 1 commit) List ✅ Lists files via Trees API Stat ✅ Returns size and SHA1 hash Copy ❌ Returns ErrNotSupported Move ❌ Returns ErrNotSupported Mkdir ❌ Directories are implicit in Git Rmdir ❌ Directories are implicit in Git

docs.title: Configuration Storage
docs.text:
Store application configuration in a Git repository for version control and audit trails:

// Write config
w, _ := backend.NewWriter(ctx, "config/app.yaml")
w.Write(configData)
w.Close()

// Read config
r, _ := backend.NewReader(ctx, "config/app.yaml")
data, _ := io.ReadAll(r)
r.Close()

docs.title: Document Management
docs.text:
Use GitHub as a document store with full version history:

// List all documents
files, _ := backend.List(ctx, "documents/")
for _, f := range files {
    fmt.Printf("%s (%d bytes)\n", f.Name, f.Size)
}

docs.title: Multi-Environment Configs
docs.text:
Use branches to manage environment-specific configurations:

// Production config
prodBackend, _ := omnistorage.New(omnistorage.Config{
    Owner:  "myorg",
    Repo:   "config",
    Branch: "production",
    Token:  token,
})

// Staging config
stagingBackend, _ := omnistorage.New(omnistorage.Config{
    Owner:  "myorg",
    Repo:   "config",
    Branch: "staging",
    Token:  token,
})

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text:
• File size: GitHub Contents API supports files up to 1MB
  • Rate limits: 5,000 requests/hour for authenticated users
  • Commits: Each write creates a commit; use batch for bulk operations

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Configuration - Detailed configuration options
  • Batch Operations - Efficient multi-file commits

docs.title: Release Notes - v0.3.0
docs.text: Release Date: 2026-07-05

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This release adds the omniskill/ package with GitHub skills for AI agents, complementing the existing storage provider functionality.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
go get github.com/plexusone/omni-github@v0.3.0

Requires Go 1.26+ and omniskill v0.10.0+.

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• New omniskill/github/ package - GitHub skill for AI agents
  • 10 tools for issues, pull requests, and code search
  • MkDocs documentation site with comprehensive guides

docs.title: GitHub Skill (omniskill/github/)
docs.text:
A new skill package providing tools for AI agents to interact with GitHub:

import "github.com/plexusone/omni-github/omniskill/github"

skill := github.New(github.Config{
    Token: os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"),
})

docs.title: Available Tools
docs.text: Tool Description list_issues List issues in a repository get_issue Get issue details create_issue Create a new issue update_issue Update an existing issue add_issue_comment Add a comment to an issue list_pull_requests List pull requests get_pull_request Get PR details add_pull_request_comment Add a comment to a PR search_code Search code across repositories search_issues Search issues and PRs

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• MkDocs site with Material theme
  • Separate guides for storage provider and skills
  • Complete tool reference with parameters

docs.title: Package Structure
docs.text:
omni-github/
├── omnistorage/         # Storage provider (existing)
│   └── ...
└── omniskill/           # NEW: Skills for AI agents
    └── github/
        ├── github.go
        └── github_test.go
